content.full_text:
December 2, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Sirs:

I am writing to you at the request of the Ontario City Council concerning clarification of certain conflict of interest issues raised by reason of a letter mailed by Ontario City Councilman Kenneth P. Waters to certain potential customers of California State Bank, where Councilman Waters was employed at the time of mailing. The letter was on California State Bank stationery and mailed in a California State Bank envelope.

I am enclosing a copy of the subject letter, which also includes Councilman Waters' City Council card and business card from California State Bank, each of which were enclosed with the letter mailed by Councilman Waters. Less than twelve (12) letters were mailed and there was one luncheon meeting with a prospective client as the result of the letter.

Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114, I request your opinion with respect to the following matters:

(1) Is Councilman Waters required to report the cost of mailing of the letter, as well as the cost of any meetings, including meals, resulting therefrom as part of his campaign statement pursuant to Section 84211 of the Government Code?

(2) If there was a mass mailing of the subject letter, are the contents of the same such that it would be subject to the provisions of Government Code Section 84305(b), requiring that the name, address and city of the candidate be shown on the outside of each piece of mail?

(3) Does the third paragraph of the subject letter, to-wit:

"There are several ways to mutually benefit from our activities and would enjoy the opportunity to discuss with you: financing, industrial development, and public sector involvement.",

Fair Political Practices Commission  
December 2, 1991  
Page Two

in any way attempt to use Councilman Waters' official position in violation of the provisions of Government Code Section 87100?

(4) Do the provisions of Government Code Section 87100 prohibit Councilman Waters from voting on matters before the Ontario City Council involving recipients of the subject letter solely by reason of the fact that the subject letter was received?

The City Council of the City of Ontario is in the process of establishing rules of conduct for City Councilmembers, and your opinion in the above matter will provide considerable assistance.

Respectfully,

COVINGTON & CROWE

BY: [signature]
SAMUEL CROWE
City Attorney for the City of Ontario

SC:sgg
Enclosure
